channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish president warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, Fox 5

item.title: Bragg to Liberty and back again: Ceremony to rechristen Army post once named for a Confederate
item.description: FORT BRAGG, N.C. (AP) — The short-lived existence of Fort Liberty came to an end Friday when the nation’s largest Army installation officially returned to its former name: Fort Bragg. Christened a century ago in honor of Confederate Gen.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN BREED and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump suggests he may not change daylight saving time
item.description: President Donald Trump indicated Thursday he may not push to end daylight saving time as Americans get ready to spring forward this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change and habitat loss, experts said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security ends collective bargaining agreement with TSA staffers, an attack on worker rights
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major attack on worker rights under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Gavin Newsom splits with Dems on trans athletes, prompting backlash from LGBTQ+ advocates
item.description: In a rebuke against the Democratic Party, California Gov. Gavin Newsom has revealed his thoughts on transgender athletes competing in girls' sporting events, and it has spurred reactions from across the state.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Princeton preppy murder victim received warning text from other brother hour before slaying
item.description: The surviving brother of Princeton murder suspect Matthew Hertgen sent a warning text message to his youngest brother about an hour before he was murdered.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: A court-appointed lawyer has advised a federal judge to let the Justice Department drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams but prevent prosecutors from ever reviving the case so it doesn't hang over him “like the proverbial Sword of Damocles.”.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico man found guilty of 'decapitating' man, 'playing soccer' with his head
item.description: Arciniega-Saenz, a homeless person at the time of the crime, stabbed Garcia 84 times with a small switchblade, the DA's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Debanking — the closure of accounts that banks consider risky, often with little notice or explanation — has become a key issue for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: "We have a lot of interest in TikTok. China is going to play a role, so hopefully China will approve of the deal," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Second measles death reported as outbreak grows in Southwest
item.description: The person was unvaccinated and a resident of Lea County, New Mexico, where at least 10 cases of the disease have been reported.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: These national parks saw record attendance in 2024, but they may not tell you about it
item.description: The National Park Service has reportedly been instructed not to draw attention to the record attendance amid staffing cuts across the agency.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Adnan Syed to stay free after judge decides on time served for his murder sentence in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Adnan Syed, whose case amassed a worldwide following of “Serial” podcast listeners, will remain free — even though his murder conviction still stands, a Baltimore judge ruled on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black box of ambulance jet that crashed in Philly wasn't recording audio, likely hadn't worked for years: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder on a plane that crash-landed in Philadelphia was not working and apparently did not work for several years, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire leads police to body found in clothing donation bin
item.description: Police are investigating a body found in a clothing donation bin in the parking lot of 70 Hudson Avenue. According to the Glens Falls Police Department, around 2:21 a.m., officers responded to an active fire at the donation bin and discovered the deceased person inside.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Abel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho court releases surviving roommates' text messages from night of student murders
item.description: Text messages between two surviving roommates of four university students killed in a home invasion stabbing have been revealed for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A fifth of US butterflies have disappeared since 2000: Study
item.description: Butterflies are disappearing from the United States at an alarming rate, with their total population declining by more than a fifth over two decades, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WFLA, NBC
